signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.24 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite escalating Middle East tensions. U.S. and Iranian forces exchanged fire in the Strait of Hormuz, driving WTI to $105.2. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains crowded; carry dominates the regime until VIX exceeds 25. Intervention risk rises on any rapid test of 160.00 as energy price surges complicate the BoJ's inflation outlook.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Oil Shock
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (specs fading hawkish bias) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for 27 May. IMF warns of a global 'working assumption' oil shock, weighing on NZD as the G8 laggard (-0.383% avg). If RBNZ delivers the hike, a short-squeeze risk is elevated against crowded spec shorts. NZD/USD at 0.5873.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Decision + Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7169 ahead of the RBA meeting on 5 May. COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 81% short — institutional vs retail divergence at extreme. While 35bp AUD carry premium supports the pair, retail is aggressively fading the institutional long. Risk of retail capitulation if RBA delivers a hawkish surprise, targeting 0.7250.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.724 vs 252d norm of -0.142 (z=+1.89σ). EUR/USD decoupling from equity weakness as the 175bp Fed-ECB carry advantage keeps the pair heavy at 1.1693. Bear steepener regime (+56.6bp 2s10s) favors USD funding. Watch for 1.1650 support if STOXX 50 (-2.00%) losses accelerate.
